skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating, updating, validating, and packaging AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, and lightweight helper scripts.
skill.summary: Create effective AI skills with structured guidance, scaffolding, validation, and packaging support.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk markers, but review found them to be Markdown backticks, placeholder text, and validation strings. No real shell execution, cryptographic code, network access, secret access, or prompt injection was found. The real risks are local filesystem writes during scaffolding and packaging every file under a selected skill directory.

medium_findings.title: Unvalidated Skill Name and Output Path Writes
medium_findings.description: The initializer builds a target directory from CLI arguments and writes SKILL.md plus example resources there. The script does not validate skill_name before composing the path, so careless agent use could create files outside the intended location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and user-controlled through CLI arguments. The behavior is legitimate scaffolding, but missing name validation creates a real filesystem safety concern.

medium_findings.title: Skill Packaging Archives All Files Recursively
medium_findings.description: The packager walks the whole skill directory and adds every file to the .skill archive. This can accidentally include secrets, large files, generated output, or symlinked files if they are present in the skill folder.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit in the code and has no exclusion list. It is normal for a packager, but it creates a clear accidental data exposure risk.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fences, inline code examples, or template text. No evidence found of subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell command execution in the executable Python files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is documentation or static template content. The reviewed scripts do not contain APIs that execute external commands.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Sensitive File Findings Are Token Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and certificate/key detections come from words such as description, metadata, allowed-tools, and example asset names. No evidence found of MD5, DES, SHA1 use, certificate parsing, or bundled key material.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain ordinary documentation and validation strings, not cryptographic functions or key files. The semantic review found no related security behavior.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Findings Are Validation Messages
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are normal validation checks, examples, and documentation guidance. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, environment dumping, or network discovery behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited code only parses SKILL.md and returns validation messages. There is no host or network reconnaissance logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unvalidated Skill Name and Output Path Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer builds a target directory from CLI arguments and writes SKILL.md plus example resources there. The script does not validate skill_name before composing the path, so careless agent use could create files outside the intended location.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are direct and user-controlled through CLI arguments. The behavior is legitimate scaffolding, but missing name validation creates a real filesystem safety concern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Skill Packaging Archives All Files Recursively
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager walks the whole skill directory and adds every file to the .skill archive. This can accidentally include secrets, large files, generated output, or symlinked files if they are present in the skill folder.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is explicit in the code and has no exclusion list. It is normal for a packager, but it creates a clear accidental data exposure risk.

content.value_statement: Creating reusable AI skills is easy to overfill with unclear instructions. This skill guides structure, scaffolding, validation, and packaging for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides authors through planning reusable scripts, references, and assets.
Provides an initializer script that scaffolds a template skill directory.
Provides a validator for basic SKILL.md frontmatter checks.
Provides a packager that creates a .skill archive after validation.
Includes reference patterns for workflows and consistent output formats.
limitations: It does not publish skills to any marketplace or remote service.
The helper scripts perform basic checks, not full security review.
The packager includes every file in the selected skill directory.
Generated templates still require human editing and testing.

use_cases.title: Create a new internal skill
use_cases.description: Plan the skill purpose, scaffold the directory, and replace template guidance with focused instructions.

use_cases.title: Improve an existing agent workflow
use_cases.description: Refactor a long prompt into reusable skill instructions, references, scripts, and assets.

use_cases.title: Prepare a skill for handoff
use_cases.description: Validate required metadata, package the folder, and document honest limitations before sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a skill for [task]. Ask for the minimum details needed, then scaffold a clear SKILL.md plan.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Repeated Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a recurring task that should become reusable.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this repeated workflow into a skill. Identify core instructions, optional references, scripts, and assets: [workflow details].

prompt_templates.title: Review Skill Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill for clarity, progressive disclosure, unnecessary files, and missing reusable resources: [skill folder or summary].

prompt_templates.title: Package and Harden a Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate, package, and audit this skill. Check metadata, file contents, accidental secrets, script risks, and marketplace readiness.

output_examples.input: Create a skill for monthly finance report generation.
output_examples.output: A focused skill plan with required instructions, finance references, validation needs, and suggested reusable report templates.

output_examples.input: Review my existing PDF workflow skill.
output_examples.output: A concise review covering trigger description quality, resource organization, missing tests, and files that should be removed.

output_examples.input: Package this finished skill for sharing.
output_examples.output: A validation result, packaging status, and a warning to remove unneeded files before distribution.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md focused on essential instructions and link deeper references only when needed.
Test bundled scripts with representative inputs before packaging the skill.
Remove placeholder files, generated output, and secrets before creating a .skill archive.
anti_patterns: Do not turn broad product documentation into a large SKILL.md file.
Do not include example directories that the final skill will never use.
Do not package a skill folder before reviewing every included file.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with focused instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging support.

faq.question: Does it write the final skill automatically?
faq.answer: It can scaffold a template and guide edits, but authors must replace placeholders with real task knowledge.

faq.question: Can it validate a skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The included validator checks basic SKILL.md frontmatter and naming rules.

faq.question: Can it package a skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The packager creates a .skill archive after validation succeeds.

faq.question: Does it perform a complete security audit?
faq.answer: No. It provides basic validation only. Security review must be done separately before publishing.

faq.question: What should authors check before sharing?
faq.answer: Authors should remove placeholders, test scripts, review all files, and confirm no sensitive data is included.
